ground_truth_summary: Minimalist vertical colour-palette / swatch fan-art poster for Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind. Title 'NAUSICAÄ' + 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' top-left in black sans-serif; 10 horizontal colour swatches stacked vertically on white background; small footer text 'THE COLORS OF' in bottom-right corner. No hex codes printed.

key_hits: Correctly identifies Nausicaä / Hayao Miyazaki / Studio Ghibli context
Correctly reads 'THE COLORS OF' bottom-right and 'NAUSICAÄ' + 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' top-left
Reasonably accurate colour descriptions (dark teal, forest green, taupe, dusty rose, cream, pale yellow, sand, sage, sky blue, azure)
key_misses: Says 'sand' for bar 7 which is more peach/apricot
Does not explicitly note absence of hex codes
judgments.justification: Strong, accurate description with correct count, text, and context. Colour names mostly on target with only minor hue drift on bar 7.

key_hits: Correct reading of all three text elements including 'THE COLORS OF.'
Good colour progression (dark teal, charcoal/forest, olive-brown, dusty rose, off-white, pale yellow, cream, mint, sky/cyan, cerulean)
key_misses: Slightly inconsistent labelling between bars (e.g., 'light cream/beige' for bar 7 vs. peach in reality)
Does not flag absence of hex codes
judgments.justification: Accurate and well-structured. Slight colour-naming imprecision but overall faithful representation.

key_hits: Correct text reading including 'THE COLORS OF.'
Notes progression from dark to light to bright blues
key_misses: Bar 7 described as 'light beige' (more peachy in reality)
No mention of hex codes absence
judgments.justification: Concise and accurate. Same minor drift on bar 7 colour as sibling Gemma variants but otherwise solid.

judgments.justification: Major object-count error — missing 2 of 10 swatches undermines the core purpose of the image. Hallucinated border framing. Text reading is fine.

Bar 10 'turquoise/medium blue' is imprecise
judgments.justification: Functional description with correct count, but loses the diacritic and slightly oversaturates the yellow. Adequate but less polished than larger models.

key_misses: Bar 1 'deep navy blue' is a misread (actually dark teal/green)
Bar 7 'pale yellow' duplicate is slightly off
Minor imprecision in bar 8 (light green) vs. mint
judgments.justification: Strong result for a 4B model — preserves diacritic, correct count, accurate text. Only the top bar colour is misidentified (navy vs. teal).

judgments.justification: Excellent colour identification and count. Only drops the umlaut; otherwise accurate and well-organized.

judgments.justification: Very thorough and accurate on count, text, and basic colours. Loses points for excessive interpretive embellishment and minor colour drift, but a strong nothink result.

Notes minimalist design with solid blocks, no gradients

judgments.justification: Top-tier result. Preserves diacritic, perfect count, accurate colour naming, clean structure. Only the most minor hue drift on bar 7.

Excellent colour sequence (deep teal/black, olive, earthy brown, dusty rose, cream/off-white, pale yellow, light yellow/cream, pale sage, sky blue, azure)

notes: Key discriminators on this image are: (a) correct swatch count of 10, (b) faithful title OCR preserving the NAUSICAÄ diaeresis, (c) accurate hue naming (especially bar 7 which is peach/apricot, not pale yellow).

Two qwen3.6-27b variants (both think and nothink) hallucinate the title spelling — 'NAUSICÄÄ' and 'NAUSICAAÄ' respectively — and rationalise the corruption with speculative design commentary. This is a notable OCR failure pattern for this model on diacritics.
gemma4-e4b undercounts to 8 swatches and qwen3.5-9b-nothink undercounts to 9 with confused self-correction; both represent object-count failures on a simple swatch stack.
qwen3.5-4b-think and the two qwen3.6-35b-a3b variants form the top tier, combining correct count, faithful diacritic preservation, and accurate hue naming.
Most models describe the bottom text with a trailing period ('THE COLORS OF.'). The dot is part of the printed design (small typographic mark), so this reading is consistent and not a hallucination.
No model explicitly noted the absence of hex codes; this is a minor missed detail across the board but not heavily weighted since the prompt-focused tasks centre on count and identification.
Gemma family has a systematic drift on bar 7 — describes it as 'sand'/'beige' rather than the peach/apricot hue — suggesting a shared colour-perception bias in that model family.
